---
title: "pre_ML"
output: html_document
date: "2025-02-25"
---
PCA
```{r}
library(robustHD)
rna_scaled <- robustScale(t(rna_matrix))  # Automatically applies robust scaling
```


```{r}
pca_plotly <- function(rna_matrix, samplesheet) {
  # Load required libraries
  library(ggplot2)
  library(plotly)
  library(RColorBrewer)
  
  # Standard scaling
  rna_scaled <- scale(t(rna_matrix))  # Transpose to make samples as rows
  
  # Perform PCA
  pca_result <- prcomp(rna_scaled, center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)
  
  # Compute percentage variance explained
  var_explained <- (pca_result$sdev^2 / sum(pca_result$sdev^2)) * 100
  pc1_var <- round(var_explained[1], 2)
  pc2_var <- round(var_explained[2], 2)
  
  # Prepare data for visualization
  pca_data <- as.data.frame(pca_result$x[, 1:2])  # Select first 2 PCs
  pca_data$class <- samplesheet$class
  pca_data$sample_id <- rownames(samplesheet)  # Add sample ID
  
  # Rename class labels
  pca_data$class <- factor(pca_data$class, 
                           levels = c("nonMT", "MT"), 
                           labels = c("Non-metastasis", "Metastasis"))
  
  # Define colors for class
  color_map <- c("Non-metastasis" = "yellow", 
                 "Metastasis" = "red")

  # Create plotly scatter plot with hover info
  p <- plot_ly(
    data = pca_data, 
    x = ~PC1, 
    y = ~PC2, 
    type = 'scatter', 
    mode = 'markers',
    marker = list(
      size = 6,  # Smaller dot size
      line = list(color = 'black', width = 1)  # Black outline
    ),
    color = ~class,
    colors = color_map,
    text = ~paste("Sample:", sample_id, "<br>Class:", class),  # Hover text
    hoverinfo = "text"
  ) %>%
    layout(
      title = "PCA Plot of RNA-seq Data",
      xaxis = list(title = paste0("PC1 (", pc1_var, "%)"), range = c(-15, 12)),  # Set X-axis range
      yaxis = list(title = paste0("PC2 (", pc2_var, "%)"), range = c(-25, 5)),  # Set Y-axis range
      legend = list(
        title = list(text = "<b>Class</b>"),  # Bold title
        orientation = "v",  # Vertical legend
        x = 1, y = 1,  # Upper right corner
        xanchor = "right",
        yanchor = "top"
      )
    )
  
  return(p)
}


```


```{r}
(pca_vst <- pca_plotly(vst_df_MT, samplesheet2_2))
```


```{r}
library(robustHD)
#Load required libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(scales)

library(robustbase)

robust_scaler <- function(x) {
  return ((x - median(x)) / Qn(x))  # Qn is a robust estimator of scale similar to IQR
}

#rna_scaled <- apply(t(rna_matrix), 2, robust_scaler)

#rna_scaled <- apply(t(rna_matrix), 2, rescale)  # Automatically scales between 0 and 1
#robust_scaler <- function(x) {
#  return ((x - median(x)) / IQR(x))
#}

#rna_scaled <- apply(t(rna_matrix), 2, robust_scaler)  # Apply robust scaling to each column
min_max_scaler <- function(x) {
  return ((x - min(x)) / (max(x) - min(x)))
}

#rna_scaled <- apply(t(rna_matrix), 2, min_max_scaler)  # Scale each column


```


```{r}
pca_plotly <- function(rna_matrix, samplesheet) {

  
  # Standard scaling
  rna_scaled <- apply(t(rna_matrix), 2, min_max_scaler)
 # Automatically applies robust scaling
  
  # Perform PCA
  pca_result <- prcomp(rna_scaled, center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)
  
  # Compute percentage variance explained
  var_explained <- (pca_result$sdev^2 / sum(pca_result$sdev^2)) * 100
  pc1_var <- round(var_explained[1], 2)
  pc2_var <- round(var_explained[2], 2)
  
  # Prepare data for visualization
  pca_data <- as.data.frame(pca_result$x[, 1:2])  # Select first 2 PCs
  pca_data$class <- samplesheet$class
  pca_data$sample_id <- rownames(samplesheet)  # Add sample ID
  
  # Rename class labels
  pca_data$class <- factor(pca_data$class, 
                           levels = c("nonMT", "MT"), 
                           labels = c("Non-metastasis", "Metastasis"))
  
  # Define colors for class
  color_map <- c("Non-metastasis" = "yellow", 
                 "Metastasis" = "red")

  # Create plotly scatter plot with hover info
  p <- plot_ly(
    data = pca_data, 
    x = ~PC1, 
    y = ~PC2, 
    type = 'scatter', 
    mode = 'markers',
    marker = list(
      size = 6,  # Smaller dot size
      line = list(color = 'black', width = 1)  # Black outline
    ),
    color = ~class,
    colors = color_map,
    text = ~paste("Sample:", sample_id, "<br>Class:", class),  # Hover text
    hoverinfo = "text"
  ) %>%
    layout(
      title = "PCA Plot of RNA-seq Data",
      xaxis = list(title = paste0("PC1 (", pc1_var, "%)"), range = c(-15, 12)),  # Set X-axis range
      yaxis = list(title = paste0("PC2 (", pc2_var, "%)"), range = c(-25, 5)),  # Set Y-axis range
      legend = list(
        title = list(text = "<b>Class</b>"),  # Bold title
        orientation = "v",  # Vertical legend
        x = 1, y = 1,  # Upper right corner
        xanchor = "right",
        yanchor = "top"
      )
    )
  
  return(p)
}


```


```{r}
(pca_vst <- pca_plotly(vst_df_MT, samplesheet2_2))
```


```{r}
vst_df_MT_T <- as.data.frame(t(vst_df_MT))
vst_df_MT_T

```


```{r}
identical(rownames(vst_df_MT_T),rownames(samplesheet2_2))

```

```{r}
samplesheet2_2$class <- factor(samplesheet2_2$class, 
                           levels = c("nonMT", "MT"), 
                           labels = c("Non-metastasis", "Metastasis"))
samplesheet2_2

```

```{r}

samplesheet2$class <- factor(samplesheet2, 
                           levels = c("nonMT", "MT", "N"), 
                           labels = c("Non-metastasis", "Metastasis", "Normal Adjacent Tissue"))
samplesheet2

```


Kolmogorov-Smirnov
```{r}

# Load necessary library
library(dplyr)

# Example RNA-seq matrix and samplesheet
# Assume rnaseq_matrix is a data frame where rows are samples and columns are genes
# Assume samplesheet is a data frame with a column "class" indicating "Metastasis" or "Non-metastasis"

# Function to perform KS test against normal distribution
ks_normal_test <- function(x) {
  # Perform KS test against normal distribution
  pval <- ks.test(x, "pnorm", mean(x, na.rm = TRUE), sd(x, na.rm = TRUE))$p.value
  return(pval >= 0.05) # TRUE if normal, FALSE otherwise
}

# Split data based on class
metastasis_samples <- samplesheet2_2$class == "Metastasis"
non_metastasis_samples <- samplesheet2_2$class == "Non-metastasis"

# Apply KS test for each gene in both groups
results <- apply(vst_df_MT_T, 2, function(gene_expr) {
  metastasis_pval <- ks_normal_test(gene_expr[metastasis_samples])
  non_metastasis_pval <- ks_normal_test(gene_expr[non_metastasis_samples])
  
  # Define the "Both" column
  both <- metastasis_pval & non_metastasis_pval
  
  return(c(Metastasis = metastasis_pval, Non_metastasis = non_metastasis_pval, Both = both))
})

# Convert results to a data frame
results_df <- as.data.frame(t(results))
results_df <- results_df %>% mutate(Gene = colnames(vst_df_MT_T)) %>% select(Gene, everything())

# Print results
print(results_df)


```


```{r}
write.table(vst_df_MT_T, file = "596_RNAmatrix.tsv", sep = "\t", row.names = FALSE, quote = FALSE)
```

```{r}
samplesheet2_2_export <- samplesheet2_2 %>% select(-Project.ID)
samplesheet2_2_export$Sample_ID <- rownames(samplesheet2_2_export)
samplesheet2_2_export <- samplesheet2_2_export %>% select(Sample_ID, class)
samplesheet2_2_export

write.table(samplesheet2_2_export, file = "samplesheet.tsv", sep = "\t", row.names = FALSE, quote = FALSE)

```
